steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank must refer back to the two paintings named in the first sentence: Creek Council Tree (1967) and Cimarron Iron Bridge (1972). The pronoun must match that antecedent in number and provide clear, specific reference.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'they were': "They" is plural, matching the two paintings. "Were" is the correct plural past-tense verb. Clear, direct reference to the antecedent. ✓
  • Choice B — 'this was': "This" is singular. The antecedent is two paintings, not one. Singular pronoun cannot refer to a plural noun phrase. ✗
  • Choice C — 'it was': "It" is singular. Same problem as B — it cannot stand in for two paintings. ✗
  • Choice D — 'some were': "Some" is an indefinite pronoun with no clear antecedent. The sentence introduces exactly two paintings; "some" implies an unspecified subset of a larger group that was never mentioned. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
